Event: Snoop lies to Michael about the purpose of their outing (claiming they're killing someone else, not him)
Explanation: Snoop deceives Michael to set up his murder, benefiting her organization at his expense.

Event: Snoop orders Michael not to bring a gun to the supposed hit
Explanation: Disarming Michael increases her safety and ability to kill him, directly endangering him.

Event: Snoop tells Michael she’ll give him a gun when they reach the location
Explanation: She maintains the ruse to keep control of the situation and preserve her plan to execute him.

Event: Michael becomes suspicious of Snoop’s inconsistent instructions about the gun
Explanation: His suspicion protects his life and indirectly reduces Snoop’s power to harm others.

Event: Michael pretends he needs to pee so Snoop will pull into an alley
Explanation: He manipulates her into a vulnerable position to save himself at her lethal expense.

Event: Michael gets the drop on Snoop and kills her
Explanation: Eliminating someone about to kill him greatly benefits his survival while harming her and her side.

Event: Middle-school Michael stares down Marlo
Explanation: His fearless stance impresses Marlo and boosts his standing, though it draws him toward a dangerous world.

Event: Marlo decides to consider recruiting Michael after seeing his nerve
Explanation: Marlo gains a promising soldier while steering Michael deeper into crime.

Event: Michael becomes a viciously effective fighter and skilled with firearms in Stanfield’s crew
Explanation: His new skills give him power and security while enabling more effective violence against others.

Event: Michael diligently takes care of and protects his brother from their sexually abusive stepfather
Explanation: He sacrifices and risks himself to keep Bug safer and more stable.

Event: Michael shows open affection and loyalty toward his younger brother
Explanation: Emotional care strengthens both his own bonds and Bug’s well-being.

Event: Michael senses Snoop’s assassination setup due to experience with hits
Explanation: He applies learned assassination tactics defensively to avoid being killed, reducing future risk to himself and others she might kill.

Event: Michael’s molestation and addict mother lead him to distrust authority and older men
Explanation: His trauma-driven distrust harms his own prospects while not clearly affecting others positively or negatively.

Event: Michael jerks away or becomes uncomfortable when most people try to touch him
Explanation: His reactions protect psychological boundaries but hinder healthy connection more for him than others.

Event: Michael becomes a stick-up man like Omar
Explanation: Robbing dealers funds him while imposing loss and fear on those he hits.

Event: Michael shoots Vinson in the knee while robbing him
Explanation: He gains money and a reputation at the cost of Vinson’s safety and livelihood.

Event: Michael distrusts the reformed Cutty for being “too friendly”
Explanation: Misreading Cutty deprives him of a positive mentor with no compensating gain.

Event: Michael chooses Marlo instead of going to a responsible adult about his problem
Explanation: Falling in with a sociopathic boss severely harms his future while offering only short-term survival/security.

Event: Michael abandons promising boxing training to support himself and Bug
Explanation: He sacrifices long-term opportunity and development to meet immediate family needs.

Event: Michael sends Bug to their aunt with all his money to keep him safe from the game
Explanation: He greatly improves Bug’s safety and prospects while impoverishing and isolating himself.

Event: Michael promises Bug more money will come in the future
Explanation: He maintains Bug’s sense of security and continuity while committing himself to continue supporting him.

Event: Michael repeatedly pounds smaller, younger Kenard
Explanation: He asserts dominance and reputation while causing physical harm to a much weaker kid.

Event: Namond refuses to take his drug package back from unconscious Kenard
Explanation: He forgoes income and favor from the package because he’s disturbed, showing empathy at his own cost.

Event: Michael stands up for Randy when other kids accuse Randy of snitching
Explanation: He risks some social standing to protect Randy from dangerous stigma.

Event: Michael visits Cutty’s gym and trains in boxing
Explanation: Training improves his physical capability and discipline with no clear harm to others.

Event: Michael leaves Cutty’s gym because Cutty’s kindness and mentoring make him uncomfortable
Explanation: Trauma-driven discomfort makes him reject a healthy support and development outlet.

Event: Michael has a one-night romantic encounter with a girl despite emotional difficulty
Explanation: It offers some mutual intimacy and normalcy with no clear harm to either.

Event: Michael sees through Snoop’s plan to kill him using lessons learned from Chris and Snoop
Explanation: He turns their own methods into protection, preserving himself and curbing their capacity to kill.

Event: Michael stares down Marlo in an early scene
Explanation: Displays of courage win Marlo’s respect and enhance Michael’s perceived strength despite future risks.

Event: People underestimate Michael’s toughness and intelligence due to his youth and quietness
Explanation: Their misjudgment benefits him tactically but often leads to their ruin, so net effect is worse for them than him.

Event: Michael kills Snoop after she underestimates him
Explanation: Her fatal miscalculation allows him to survive at her total expense.

Event: Michael kneecaps Vinson after Vinson dismisses him as a threat
Explanation: Vinson’s underestimation lets Michael rob and injure him for gain.

Event: Michael wins a paintball-gun training exercise against experienced killers Chris and Snoop
Explanation: He gains skill, respect, and confidence without real harm to others in a simulated environment.

Event: Michael accepts training with Chris and Snoop to become more dangerous
Explanation: He increases his own lethality and standing while setting up more future harm to others.

Event: Michael refuses to kill a child during the Junebug house massacre
Explanation: He preserves the child’s life but risks angering his superiors and complicating his position.
